problem: Find the remainder when $3 \times 13 \times 23 \times 33 \times \ldots \times 183 \times 193$ is divided by $5$.

classified_questions.question: What is the definition of the remainder when a number is divided by another number? How can we calculate it?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the remainder when $3$ is divided by $5$? How about $13$, $23$, $33$, and so on? Do you notice any patterns?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can you determine the remainder when $3 \times 13$ is divided by $5$? How about $3 \times 13 \times 23$, and so on? Is there a pattern that emerges?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Based on the pattern you observed, what can you say about the remainder when $3 \times 13 \times 23 \times \ldots \times 183 \times 193$ is divided by $5$? Can you express it using exponents?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we simplify $3^{20} \pmod{5}$? Can you apply any properties or rules of exponents to simplify it further?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final result? What is the remainder when $3 \times 13 \times 23 \times \ldots \times 183 \times 193$ is divided by $5$?
classified_questions.label: b
